approvals.note: All insight lines, the directional tag and band notes are copy awaiting sign-off.

directionalTag: Directional read from 10 questions. The full scan confirms it in your numbers.

capture.A: When a quote request lands and you cannot get to it, what tends to happen?
capture.B: When a booking enquiry lands and you cannot get to it, what tends to happen?
capture.C: When an order or enquiry lands and you cannot get to it, what tends to happen?

convert.A: After you send a quote, how does the follow-up happen?
convert.B: After someone enquires about a booking, how does the follow-up happen?
convert.C: After someone abandons an order or enquiry, how does the follow-up happen?

return.A: How do past customers come back for repeat work?
return.B: How do past customers come back to rebook?
return.C: How do past customers come back for a repeat order?

steps.stem: What kind of business are we scanning?
steps.helper: Pick the closest. Sage already knows the leak patterns for your world.
steps.insight: Every sector leaks in its own pattern. Sage already knows yours.

steps.stem: Roughly how many new enquiries reach you in a typical week?
steps.helper: Calls, forms, messages, walk-ins. A rough count is fine.
steps.insight: The more enquiries you handle, the more a small capture gap quietly costs.

steps.stem: Which of these describe how enquiries reach you today?
steps.helper: Select every one that sounds familiar.
steps.insight: If the phone is the only reliable door in, every missed ring is a customer walking to the next name on the list. That usually points to a capture problem, not a marketing problem.

options.label: The phone is our main line and we cannot always answer
After hours, calls go to voicemail
WhatsApp, texts and DMs sometimes get missed
Web enquiries come in but replies can be slow
There is no easy way to book or order online
Every enquiry gets answered quickly, one way or another

steps.stem: When an enquiry lands and you cannot get to it, what tends to happen?
steps.helper: Be honest. This is where most leaks start.
steps.insight: A missed enquiry rarely calls back. It just becomes someone else's booked job.

options.label: It goes to voicemail and often we never hear back
Some just get lost in the day
We call back when we get a chance, sometimes next day
Nothing logs it, so we cannot chase what we did not catch
An automatic reply holds them until we can respond

steps.stem: After you send a quote, how does the follow-up happen?
steps.helper: The gap between sent and won is where quotes go cold.
steps.insight: Most quotes do not die from a no. They die from silence. Sequenced follow-up is usually the fastest money on the table.

options.label: We send it once and wait to hear back
We chase if we remember, when things are quiet
Someone chases manually, one at a time
Every one enters a set follow-up sequence

steps.stem: After a job goes well, how do you collect a review?
steps.helper: The proof that wins the next customer.
steps.insight: Every happy customer is a five-star review you did not ask for. Most businesses ask for none.

options.label: We ask by hand, one message at a time
Every finished job triggers a review request

steps.stem: How do past customers come back for repeat work?
steps.helper: Select every one that is true today.
steps.insight: Past customers are worth more than every cold lead combined. Most are sitting in your database, waiting to be asked.

options.label: They come back on their own, or they do not
We have no reminder or win-back going out
We chase from a manual list when we think of it
We treat repeat work as one-off jobs
Automated reminders and win-backs run in the background

steps.stem: If someone asked ChatGPT, Google or Perplexity for a business like yours nearby, would you show up?
steps.helper: AI search is where the next recommendation happens.
steps.insight: Your buyers are already asking AI who to call. If you are not in the answer, you are invisible right where the decision gets made.

options.label: Maybe on Google, nowhere in AI search
We are listed but it is thin and out of date
We show up well across Google and AI answers

steps.stem: Which of these still run on someone's memory or manual work?
steps.helper: Select every one that is done by hand today.
steps.insight: Anything that lives in a person's head leaks the day they are busy, off, or gone. Systems do not forget.

options.label: Answering the same questions over and over
Most of this is already automated

steps.stem: If Sage could take one thing off your plate first, what would it be?
steps.helper: This tells us where to start.
steps.insight: We fix the leak that is costing you most first, then build out from there.

options.label: Win more of the quotes I send
Get the reviews I have earned
Get found in search and AI
Win back my time from admin

captureLossRate.note: Directional share of weekly enquiries assumed missed, by capture severity. Not a promise.

taskTimes.note: Likely time estimates, not measured. The full scan measures the real numbers.
